guardrails: JSON Schema is the docs-facing and fixture-facing source of truth.
The Rust projection is consumed by the CLI and SDK but must not become contract authority.
CLI runtime remains Rust-first and targets Overgate through SDK contracts.
Fixtures must preserve final output-envelope and Overgate-only validation shapes.
Profiles and credential references must be secret-free and environment-scoped before request construction.
Signer handoff returns signature refs only and never exposes raw key material.
Command lifecycle, exit classes, diagnostics, and capability snapshots must be rendered from the canonical output envelope.
Phase 1 bootstrap commands must construct signed SDK/Overgate envelopes and must not call Overtenant, Overpass, Overkey, Overregistry, Overqueue, or Overwatch directly.
Canonical idempotency fingerprints must include environment, endpoint identity, tenant, actor, command, target, canonical payload hash, expected current state, reason, and schema version.
Retry and timeout policy must stay bounded, classify retryable transport/platform failures, and keep denials non-retryable.
Error decode records must expose stable reason codes, retry classes, exit classes, and remediation hints without raw internal errors.
Local idempotency cache records must be scoped to profile/environment, owner-only, inspectable/resettable, and free of private payloads.
Phase 7 node status records must be profile-scoped, credential-checked, capability-ref backed, and free of direct node access.
Phase 7 workload execution timelines must use Overgate/Overqueue/Oversched/Overlease/Overrun/Overwatch refs and expose trace-linked diagnostics only.
Phase 7 log/result retrieval must expose authorized refs, bounded streaming or polling plans, and redacted outputs without private storage paths.
Phase 8 policy dry-run commands must remain dry-run/read-only and route through SDK/Overgate contracts without direct policy-service access.
Phase 8 package validation commands must expose schema/signature/hash/dependency/permission/SBOM/provenance/policy compatibility refs without direct package-store access.
Phase 8 usage, receipt, ledger, and dispute read commands must expose authorized read-model refs without direct meter, ledger, dispute, or payment mutation access.
Phase 8 accounting outputs must not encode pricing, revenue, customer-count, market-volume, blockchain, or NFT assumptions.
Phase 9 product integration workflows for Docdex, Mcoda, and Codali must route through CLI/SDK/Overgate recipes and authorized refs, not direct internal APIs.

Phase 9 Mcoda product workflows must keep model, provider, resource, tool-boundary, budget, and usage metadata dynamic without hardcoded model, node, paid-service, raw HTTP, or private-storage assumptions.
Phase 9 Codali product workflows must expose repository context, logs, artifacts, structured results, retry/repair boundaries, and per-agent-phase usage through authorized refs only.
Phase 9 CI automation profiles must use explicit ci profile kinds and short-lived or mounted credential refs with stable secret-free JSON output and no ambient persistent keychain defaults.
Phase 10 release-readiness output must validate contract snapshots, help text, exit classes, reason-code families, JSON/human output, and error decoding without changing earlier command contracts.

Phase 10 security validation must keep credential storage, signer handoff, diagnostics, logs, results, and handoff output secret-free with no raw keys, tokens, signatures, private payloads, decrypted content, unsafe endpoints, or cross-tenant shortcuts.
Phase 10 phase-availability validation must keep unavailable later services denied with stable not_available_in_phase reason codes and no private shortcuts.
Phase 10 Phase 7/13 handoff evidence must not authorize high-risk governance, compliance, incident, break-glass, migration, or backbone operations before owning contracts exist.
Integration Harness Phase 2 contracts must keep fixture, scenario, run-record, assertion, golden-trace, and artifact-bundle schemas as canonical JSON Schema sources with Rust projection checks only.
Integration Harness Phase 2 fixture credentials must be visibly test_only and must reject raw key material, non-local profiles, unsafe timeouts, unsupported phases, and raw secret artifact bundles.

Integration Harness Phase 2 artifact bundles must name redacted log, Overwatch export, CLI output, API payload envelope, stack health, and fixture version refs, and reject private keys, tokens, signatures, encrypted RAG content, private payloads, and fixture key material.
